fullyImplemented: User workspace operational routes are dense and workflow-oriented.
Reports keep summary, drill-down, and export actions visible.
Reconciliation import now shows explicit next actions, row issues, success state, and re-import flow.
partiallyImplemented: Some non-core role areas still rely on generic or placeholder route families outside the main user workspace.
A few empty states remain descriptive rather than deeply action-guided.
phase2: Finish non-user workspace operational shells to remove placeholder-driven experiences.

fullyImplemented: Sales document families render through shared preview and PDF contracts.
Invoice, proforma, delivery note, credit note, and debit note proof was regenerated successfully.
Document flows link into payments, journals, and VAT outputs.
partiallyImplemented: Purchase-family depth is present but not fully re-audited in this hardening pass for every state transition and reversal edge case.
phase2: Broaden purchase-family parity review and add more explicit reversal-state evidence.

fullyImplemented: Dedicated chart, journal, ledger, opening balance, and report routes exist.
Discount posting, journal linkage, VAT detail reporting, and immutable posted-journal discipline are enforced.
Journal register exposes linked document context and source-aware actions.
partiallyImplemented: Cash-flow depth remains lighter than the rest of the reporting suite because reconciliation linkage is still operationally shallow compared with ledgers and VAT.
The existing audit tool still flags bill and expense account classification as too shallow on /workspace/bills/new.
phase2: Deepen cash-flow and purchase-side classification intelligence.

fullyImplemented: Inventory stock, adjustments, and accounting linkage are real and persisted.
Inventory insufficiency blocking and stock-linked journal behavior were already validated in Phase 1 closure.
partiallyImplemented: Delivery-note stock policy and every reversal edge were not re-tested in this hardening pass.
The audit tool still records one inventory logic finding, so the inventory area is strong but not perfect.
phase2: Expand delivery-note and return-flow evidence around stock reservation versus reduction policy.

fullyImplemented: Dedicated report routes exist for P&L, trial balance, balance sheet, cash flow, receivables aging, payables aging, and VAT summary.
VAT received and VAT paid detail drill-down now exists in the reporting contract and UI.
JSON export is available from the accounting report screen.
partiallyImplemented: Cash-flow completeness still depends on further reconciliation maturity.
Cross-report consistency has been validated for key flows but not exhaustively for every legacy preview dataset.

fullyImplemented: Import accepts file upload, pasted rows, CSV, TSV, XLSX, and XLS inputs.

Smart mapping suggests transaction date, value date, reference, invoice number, payment reference, customer, company, amount, subtotal, VAT, currency, quantity, item description, running balance, and account/category fields.
Mapping can be overridden manually and surfaces missing required fields, duplicate mappings, and unmapped headers.
Preview distinguishes valid rows, blocking issues, warnings, and post-import behavior before any write.
Execution now returns imported, skipped, and failed counts plus generated record summaries and warnings.
Import logs persist source, operator label, row counts, summary, warnings, errors, and generated records in the browser session.
Successful import hands the user back into reconciliation and candidate matching instead of a dead confirmation state.
partiallyImplemented: Import destinations beyond reconciliation, such as bulk contacts and item imports, are still not implemented as first-class modules.
Import logs are durable inside the browser session, but there is not yet a unified backend log register for all import destinations.
missing: Dedicated product and item import workflow.
phase2: Expand the same hardened import contract into contacts, items, and other accounting-supporting datasets.

fullyImplemented: Phase 1 invoice, payment, VAT, reporting, journal, and inventory proof remains green in the backend workflow suite.
Import-to-reconciliation handoff now works in preview mode with runtime screenshots and JSON proof.
partiallyImplemented: Authenticated backend reconciliation with workspace credentials was not available in this shell, so runtime proof used preview mode rather than a privileged company session.
blocked: No backend workspace credentials were present for a live authenticated reconciliation pass.
phase2: Add authenticated runtime proof for company-scoped bank imports and reconciliation closeout.

fullyImplemented: VAT reporting, VAT detail drill-down, document VAT metadata, and ZATCA-aware rendering remain active.
Customer-oriented VAT outputs are reflected in reports and document proof.
partiallyImplemented: The full customer-origin decision matrix was not re-executed end-to-end during this import hardening pass.
phase2: Extend VAT decision evidence across broader cross-border and multi-country scenarios.

fullyImplemented: Primary operational flows provide next actions and workflow continuity in invoices, payments, reports, and reconciliation import.
Import validation failures now preserve a direct fix-and-retry path.
partiallyImplemented: Placeholder-heavy assistant and agent route families still weaken overall cross-workspace workflow continuity.
phase2: Complete assistant and agent workspace route families so workflow intelligence is consistent outside the user workspace.

fullyImplemented: Journal rows expose linked documents and source-aware actions.
Audit trail and review surfaces exist and remain routable.
Import logs and reconciliation state are visible in the user workflow.
partiallyImplemented: Import logging is not yet unified with a central backend audit trail across all module families.
phase2: Unify import and reconciliation actions into one persistent audit-trail stream.

globalDeviations: The built-in audit tool still reports 20 placeholder routes, mostly in admin, assistant, and agent workspaces.
Phase 1 core user workspace is substantially more complete than the non-core role workspaces.
